mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates above 8.5% indicate weak labor market conditions that depress consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) signals robust economic activity that boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with GDPC1 growth (Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market that drives consumer confidence and equity valuations higher.
failure_condition: Interaction between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to explain SPX returns (p > 0.10 for joint effect in regression).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing activity that supports corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO (Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing equity discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising consumer spending (RSAFS) indicate robust demand conditions that drive S&P 500 returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction between HOUST and RSAFS fails to explain SPX returns (p > 0.15 for joint effect in regression).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening cycle that raises borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.
